notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Waited first; ready. Inspected get_case and list_evidence: only five initial case-packet files, no filings or submitted evidence. Read all five full evidence items. Record theory: pUSD appears to satisfy proposition as Polymarket USD, standard ERC-20, Polygon mainnet, backed by USDC, transferable, 6 decimals, used as collateral token for all trading; official contracts page identifies pUSD CollateralToken proxy at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB; changelog/migration guide indicate Apr. 17, 2026 announcement of new collateral token and Apr. 28, 2026 CLOB V2 live/migration. Strongest defense: this is merely technical collateral/settlement infrastructure, not a public token launch. Response: condition does not require an investment token, exchange listing, or non-collateral purpose; it requires official fungible token released by Polymarket and publicly communicated through official channels; pUSD is not NFT, promotional, points, or collaboration token. Provenance/custody limitations: captures were post-run attorney-discovered; no server-dated headers, archive snapshots, or publication history preserved; actual market URL/id absent; no secondary reporting yet. Native investigation: opened official Polymarket docs URLs listed in evidence (concepts/pusd, resources/contracts, changelog, v2-migration) and current pages remain consistent with packet summaries, but opening phase does not allow evidence submission so no new source is being cited as record proof. Updated /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source map, evidence plan, adverse checks, and unresolved gaps. Planned opening: forward-looking, record-based, candid about gaps, asks council to treat pUSD as qualifying official fungible token if official docs/chain evidence are credited by preponderance.

Opening:defendant work notes. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence; five case-packet files only, no later submitted evidence/exhibits. Read all five files via read_evidence_range. ev_9f790b70e7f8_market-page contains condition text and notes no real Polymarket market URL/id; elements: fungible, official Polymarket release, official public communication, exclusions for NFT/promotional/points/collaboration launches. ev_2c067be8800a_primary-evidence and ev_ce624e6c59c1_official-source-record summarize post-run official docs: pUSD described as Polymarket USD, standard ERC-20 collateral token on Polygon, backed by USDC, transferable, 6 decimals; official contracts page allegedly lists pUSD CollateralToken proxy 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB; changelog/migration pages allegedly describe April 2026 CLOB V2 with new collateral token. ev_38c7491e77a8_unresolved-record-gaps expressly identifies gaps: no market URL/id, no pre-run official market/API capture, no server-dated headers/immutable archives/exact page publication histories, and core semantic issue whether this is an official token launch or technical collateral introduced with CLOB V2. ev_8676b8beeed6_secondary-reporting says no secondary reporting captured yet. Native web investigation during opening searched: site:docs.polymarket.com pUSD Polymarket USD CLOB V2 migration April 28 2026; Polymarket pUSD official token April 2026. Found current official/docs leads matching packet: docs.polymarket.com/v2-migration, /changelog, /concepts/pusd, help.polymarket.com article Polymarket Exchange Upgrade: April 28, 2026, and Polygonscan address. Also found secondary leads (The Defiant, KuCoin, Techflow, Webopedia, Bitcoin.com). These are private source targets only for later evidence because openings cannot admit evidence and unadmitted URLs will not be cited as record proof. Defense strategy: concede record supports pUSD as a fungible ERC-20 collateral asset, but argue plaintiff still must prove the proposition’s “official token launch” element; current record is summary-heavy, post-run-captured, and has custody/timing gaps. Need later admissible full captures, headers/archives/version history, on-chain data, and any official Polymarket distinction between collateral migration and public token/governance launch.

Merits argument opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Refreshed get_case/list_evidence after defendant opening. Record now has both openings, initial five packet files, no arguments or submitted evidence before plaintiff submissions. Defendant theory: pUSD may be official platform collateral/ERC-20 but not an official token launch; emphasizes source-chain and timing gaps. Submitted four focused official Polymarket documentation captures as evidence: ev_f34b5612c619_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-f34b5612c619 (pUSD page, Polymarket USD is collateral token used for all trading, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, backed by USDC, transferable, 6 decimals, no current external listing plans); ev_eb5be3872a12_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-eb5be3872a12 (contracts page, single source of truth, pUSD CollateralToken proxy and impl); ev_04730ea1807f_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-04730ea1807f (changelog Apr 17/A Apr 28 2026, new collateral token pUSD, pUSD replaces USDC.e, CLOB V2 live on production); ev_0979024ce767_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-0979024ce767 (migration guide, Polymarket shipped new collateral token Polymarket USD/pUSD, CLOB V2 live Apr 28, pUSD replaces USDC.e, UI wraps automatically). Native/adverse searches: queries for Polymarket pUSD token launch/collateral; found confirming secondary reports and an adverse Blockhead framing that pUSD is collateral only, not tradeable, separate from POLY governance token. Did not submit secondary sources because official Polymarket docs are stronger and quota-limited; can use in rebuttal if semantic framing remains decisive. Remaining gaps: no immutable archive/publication history, no server headers, no exact market URL/id. Planned argument: offer initial case packet and four new official captures; concede preservation gaps; argue under condition text that pUSD qualifies because it is fungible ERC-20, officially released by Polymarket, publicly communicated through official docs/changelog/migration pages, before Jan 1 2027, and none of the exclusions apply.

Arguments:defendant work notes. At opportunity start, rescanned get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff added four official Polymarket text captures in arguments: ev_f34b5612c619 pUSD page, ev_eb5be3872a12 contracts page, ev_04730ea1807f changelog, ev_0979024ce767 CLOB V2 migration guide. Read all four via read_evidence_range. Adverse facts: pUSD is standard ERC-20, transferable, 6 decimals, Polygon, backed by USDC, used for all trading; contracts page lists pUSD CollateralToken proxy; changelog/migration guide date upgrade to Apr. 28, 2026. Defense-friendly facts in same evidence: pUSD page says day-to-day nothing changes and pUSD is the technical settlement layer underneath the same experience; it says pUSD is designed to function within Polymarket and there are no current plans to list it on external exchanges. Migration/changelog frame event as CLOB V2/integration upgrade, wrapping USDC.e, UI-handled collateral migration, paused trading, open orders wiped, SDK/order-struct changes. All plaintiff captures note they are focused text extractions, not server-dated publication-history captures. Native investigation found official Help Center article at https://help.polymarket.com/en/articles/14762452-polymarket-exchange-upgrade-april-28-2026. It frames the event as Polymarket Exchange Upgrade; key details say for most users nothing, balance carries over, one-time approval; pUSD section says migrating from USDC.e to pUSD as collateral token, day to day nothing changes, pUSD is technical layer underneath. Submitted this as defendant evidence; accepted as ev_57084a7a538c_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-57084a7a538c, sha256 57084a7a538cfc05aecec1637f18a3d7c33a6120cc118df8fafb328e80c4c5b9. Argument plan: concede literal fungibility/official docs but argue plaintiff proved collateral migration/infrastructure upgrade, not the market-resolution concept of official token launch. Offer defendant evidence plus plaintiff pUSD/changelog/migration/gap evidence. Include a concise technical report/search ledger. Remaining gaps: no real market URL/id, no pre-run market API/page capture, no immutable archive/server header/publication history; but semantic issue remains decisive.

Plaintiff rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Refreshed from prompt and read/stat defendant's new evidence DX-1: ev_57084a7a538c_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-57084a7a538c, official Polymarket Help Center article titled Polymarket Exchange Upgrade: April 28, 2026. Full 3195-byte read. DX-1 says on Apr. 28, 2026 at ~11:00 UTC Polymarket is rolling out coordinated exchange-stack upgrade: new smart contracts, rewritten order book, and a new collateral token; users need to approve conversion of balance to pUSD and approve trading on upgraded contracts; pUSD is backed 1:1 by USDC with backing enforced onchain. It has a section 'A new collateral token: Polymarket USD (pUSD)' saying Polymarket is migrating from USDC.e to Polymarket USD (pUSD) as the collateral token; pUSD is a standard ERC-20 token on Polygon; FAQ says pUSD is the new collateral token for Polymarket, a regular ERC-20 on Polygon, convertible back to USDC. Rebuttal plan: do not submit new evidence; use DX-1 against defense semantic point. Defense relies on 'technical layer underneath' and 'day to day nothing changes,' but those describe user experience and automatic wrapping, not an exclusion. Condition does not exclude collateral or technical settlement tokens. DX-1 supplies the user-facing official public communication and confirms launch/conversion timing. Updated /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Surrebuttals:defendant work notes. At start of opportunity, called get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff filed rebuttal but submitted no new evidence; plaintiff relied on defendant's DX-1 Help Center article and existing official docs. Plaintiff's main rebuttal point: DX-1 confirms official public communication, pUSD as new collateral token/regular ERC-20 on Polygon, users approve conversion of balances to pUSD and upgraded contracts, and 'technical layer underneath' describes user experience rather than disqualifying the token. No new source issue requiring outside search; dispute is classification/weight. Planned surrebuttal: answer strongest point directly. Defense does not argue for a new exclusion; defense argues plaintiff must prove the event is a market-resolution 'launch' of an official token, and plaintiff's own evidence frames it as exchange upgrade/collateral migration/user-interface abstraction. Key facts to stress: ordinary users were told day-to-day nothing changes; balances carry over; UI handles conversion; pUSD designed to function within Polymarket and no current external listing plan; docs are integration/migration guides. No new evidence submitted in surrebuttal.

Plaintiff closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Refreshed full record with get_case/list_evidence before closing; evidence list includes initial packet, four plaintiff official docs captures, defendant Help Center capture. Closing phase permits no new evidence. Defendant surrebuttal concedes DX-1 confirms pUSD is a new ERC-20 collateral token used in the Apr. 28 exchange upgrade; defense classifies it as collateral mechanism rather than official token launch. Final synthesis: strongest proof is PX-5 pUSD page (Polymarket USD, collateral token used for all trading, standard ERC-20, backed by USDC, transferable, 6 decimals), PX-6 contracts page (single source of truth, pUSD CollateralToken), PX-7 changelog (Apr. 17 new collateral token pUSD; Apr. 28 CLOB V2 live), PX-8 migration guide (Polymarket shipped new collateral token Polymarket USD/pUSD; live Apr. 28), and DX-1/PX-R1 (official Help Center user-facing exchange upgrade; new collateral token; users approve conversion to pUSD). Weaknesses remain: no immutable archive/page history, captures are June 30, no market URL/id. Closing plan: argue preponderance and condition text. Defense asks council to add unstated requirements: standalone launch, external listing, governance/speculative token, or non-collateral purpose. The condition excludes NFTs, promotions, points, and collaboration/community tokens only; it does not exclude collateral/settlement tokens. Updated /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closings:defendant work notes. At start of closing, performed get_case and list_evidence. No new evidence after rebuttal; plaintiff closing filed but added no source material. Plaintiff's closing frames final issue as classification, argues defense conceded pUSD is new ERC-20 collateral token, rule does not exclude collateral tokens, official sources consistently describe pUSD as new collateral token publicly rolled out before deadline, and provenance gaps do not overcome five official Polymarket sources. Defense closing plan: concede fungibility and official collateral rollout; focus on burden/classification. Plaintiff must prove that CLOB V2 exchange upgrade/collateral migration equals market-resolution 'launched an official fungible token.' Use record facts: DX-1 and PX-5 say day-to-day nothing changes and pUSD is the technical layer underneath; pUSD is designed to function within Polymarket and no current external listing plan; migration guide describes UI-handled wrapping and API wrap() calls, balances carried over, downtime/open orders/SDK changes. Argue 'launch' must do work and cannot be reduced to any official ERC-20 contract or settlement wrapper. Source-chain gaps remain, but semantic classification is decisive.
